calibration_nodes.text: [Beacon Scan 1/2] In your limited free time, where do you usually hang out in the digital battlefield?

options.text: I don't really game, prefer real life/work/TV shows

calibration_nodes.text: [Beacon Scan 2/2] If you were thrown into a 10-person team to complete a major project, what role do you usually play?

options.text: The architect/captain who calls the shots
The support who covers the rear and clears mines

question_pool.text: [MOBA Scenario] In team fights, I prefer picking assassins/ADCs who can 1v5 rather than pure tank supports who initiate and absorb damage.

options.text: Strongly agree (I am the absolute core)
Strongly disagree (I like sacrificing for the team)

question_pool.text: [Clutch Scenario] In a 1v3 clutch, I trust my own aim to actively seek opportunities rather than hiding in a corner waiting for teammate callouts.
[Business Scenario] When investing in a restaurant, I prefer a 50-year-old traditional brand with strict rules over a newly established, aggressively styled pop-up.
[Social Scenario] At a party with strangers, I'm usually the one who actively seeks topics and controls the conversation rhythm.
[Life Philosophy] As long as the goal is achieved, using some less dignified or opportunistic methods (as long as not illegal) is acceptable.
[Travel Scenario] When traveling, I plan every day's itinerary down to the hour. I don't like spontaneous 'go-with-the-flow' trips full of unknowns.
[Comeback Scenario] When the game is heavily behind and teammates start surrender votes, I not only don't agree but get more motivated to play harder.
[Sports Preference] If I must choose a PE class, I'd pick boxing/weightlifting emphasizing absolute strength and power over fencing/gymnastics emphasizing flexibility and technique.
[Workplace Pressure] When the boss suddenly scraps half-executed plans and demands a complete redo with new ideas, I can immediately stop complaining and quickly switch gears.
